input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** ŌĆö use `AppThemeBinding` to declare light/dark values inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The markup extension takes `Light` and `Dark` parameters. MAUI resolves the correct value at runtime and **updates automatically** when the user toggles the system theme ŌĆö no event handling needed.

---

**C# alternative** ŌĆö use `SetAppTheme<T>()` or `SetAppThemeColor()`:

```csharp
// For Color properties (convenience method)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For any property type (generic version)
page.SetAppTheme<Color>(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Colors.White,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

button.SetAppTheme<Color>(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Auto-updates** | Both approaches react to OS theme changes at runtime |
| **Default theme** | Falls back to `Light` if the OS reports `Unspecified` |
| **Override globally** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For larger apps, define colors in `AppTheme`-keyed resources in `App.xaml` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` to keep values centralized |

For anything beyond a handful of colors, move your palette into `App.xaml` resource dictionaries so you update values in one place instead of on every element.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to make a ContentPage theme-aware. This is a knowledge/tutorial question, not a code modification task on an existing repo. I'll provide a concise but complete answer with XAML and C# examples.
